tasks.title: Decide social credentials vault permission gate
tasks.description:
Decide whether the new Social Credentials Vault (G-4) reuses the existing `SOCIAL_SETTINGS_MANAGE` permission (mirroring how the AI Vault reuses `AI_SETTINGS_MANAGE` instead of a dedicated permission, per packages/schemas/src/enums/permission.enum.ts) or needs a new `SOCIAL_CREDENTIALS_MANAGE` member. Default recommendation: reuse `SOCIAL_SETTINGS_MANAGE` for consistency. If a new permission is required, add it to `PermissionEnum` with JSDoc matching the existing entries' style. Document the decision inline as a code comment on whichever route file ends up gating the vault (T-026/T-027).

DECISION (2026-07-02): reuse `SOCIAL_SETTINGS_MANAGE`. It already gates the settings route that owns `make_webhook_url` (apps/api/src/routes/social/admin/settings/*), which the vault migration (T-024) retires -- so the vault is a natural extension of the same permission surface, exactly mirroring the AI Vault reusing `AI_SETTINGS_MANAGE` instead of minting `AI_CREDENTIALS_MANAGE`. No new PermissionEnum member added. The inline code comment lands in T-026 when the vault route file is created.

subtasks.title: Compare AI vault's permission reuse precedent vs a dedicated permission
Add PermissionEnum member if a new permission is chosen
Document the decision inline near the gating route

qualityGate.tests: n/a

Add a placeholder to `apps/api/.env.example`. Run `pnpm env:check:registry` to confirm sync. Do NOT set the real value anywhere yet — that is a separate operational step (T-033) requiring Coolify access.

Add Zod field in apps/api/src/utils/env.ts

tasks.title: Create social_credentials Drizzle schema
tasks.description: Create `packages/db/src/schemas/social/social_credentials.dbschema.ts`, mirroring `ai_provider_credentials.dbschema.ts` file-for-file: columns `id` (uuid pk), `key` (varchar, unique per active row — 'make_webhook_url'|'make_api_key'|'ai_social_key'|'operator_pin'), `ciphertext` (text), `iv` (varchar 32), `authTag` (varchar 32), `label` (varchar, optional), `createdAt`/`updatedAt`, `deletedAt`/`deletedById` (soft-delete, FK to users with onDelete:set null). Add a partial unique index on `key` `WHERE deleted_at IS NULL`. Export `InsertSocialCredential`/`SelectSocialCredential` types. Write a schema unit test verifying column types and the partial unique index exist.

subtasks.title: Define table columns mirroring ai_provider_credentials
Add partial unique index on key where deleted_at IS NULL
Export from db package barrel file
Write schema unit test

tasks.title: Create social_credential_audit Drizzle schema

Add index on `(key, createdAt desc)` and on `actorId`. Export `InsertSocialCredentialAudit`/`SelectSocialCredentialAudit` types. Write a schema unit test verifying columns and indexes.

qualityGate.tests: pass — 676 tests across service-core social suite (133 dispatch service + 9 edge-cases + rest) and apps/api cron+social routes. dispatchTarget()/dispatchPostNow() no longer touch social_settings for the webhook URL at all — webhookUrl is now a REQUIRED caller-supplied param (mirrors makeApiKey), sourced by both apps/api call sites (cron job + publish-now.ts) via getDecryptedSocialCredential({ key: 'make_webhook_url' }).

tasks.decisionNote: SEED-ROW REMOVAL DEFERRED — 2026-07-03. Discovered a SECOND, unrelated read-site of `make_webhook_url` from social_settings: SocialPostService.getDashboard() (packages/service-core/src/services/social/social-post.service.ts:2175) reads it purely to compute the admin dashboard's `makeWebhookConfigured` display flag. Removing the seed row (as the task literally asked) would silently break that indicator (always false) since service-core cannot reach the vault directly (apps/api-only isolation, same principle as the AI vault). Asked the user via AskUserQuestion whether to (A) also migrate this second read-site to vault-sourced via caller injection, (B) leave the seed row in place for now, or (C) remove it and accept the dashboard regression. Got no response (user AFK) — per this session's standing conservative-default guardrail, chose (B): the dispatch-time credential read IS fully vault-sourced now (the security-critical part of G-4), but the `make_webhook_url` row was NOT removed from packages/seed/src/required/socialAutomation.seed.ts, so social_settings still carries a plaintext copy alongside the vault credential until this is explicitly resolved. Needs a decision next session before T-042 (legacy env var + settings cleanup) can be considered complete for this key.

qualityGate.tests: pass — 8 new tests in test/routes/social/admin/credentials.test.ts (handler-capture-via-mock pattern, mirrors test/routes/ai/credentials.test.ts). Covers: list registration, masked-item shape (no ciphertext/iv/authTag/plaintext), includeDeleted true/false default, service-error propagation; create registration, actorId+ipAddress passthrough, masked mutation result (no plaintext/ciphertext), duplicate-key error propagation.

tasks.decisionNote: Followed the LOCAL social/admin/* convention (one file per route + index.ts assembling, e.g. hashtag-sets/footers) rather than the AI vault's single monolithic file — this repo's social admin routes are already structured that way, and it fits better than a literal file-for-file AI-vault mirror. Reused SOCIAL_SETTINGS_MANAGE per T-001's decision (no new permission). No @repo/schemas equivalent existed for social credentials (unlike AiCredentialCreateInputSchema etc.) so request/response Zod schemas are defined locally in the route files, same pattern as CredentialListQuerySchema in the AI vault's route file. Did NOT test a real HTTP 403 — this codebase's route tests for this style (list.ts/create.ts factories) universally mock out createAdminRoute/createAdminListRoute and trust the shared adminAuthMiddleware (already covered elsewhere), matching test/routes/ai/credentials.test.ts precedent exactly. Rotate/update/delete admin routes are T-027, not touched here.

Covers: rotate/update/delete route registration, actorId+ipAddress passthrough (each service call asserted toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1), consistent with the one-audit-row-per-mutation guarantee already proven at the service layer in T-018/T-019's own tests), masked mutation results (no plaintext/ciphertext/newPlaintext), and NOT_FOUND propagation on a non-existent key (maps to 404 via the shared ServiceError -> HTTP mapping).

tasks.decisionNote: Used `{key}` path params (Hono/OpenAPI brace syntax, matching T-026 and the rest of this codebase) rather than the task description's literal `:key` (Express-style) — same convention gap as T-026 vs its `admin/social/credentials.ts` literal path. Found and fixed a real bug in the handler-capture-mock test harness while writing these: PATCH /{key} and DELETE /{key} share an identical path string, and the mock keyed captured handlers by path alone (mirroring test/routes/ai/credentials.test.ts's existing pattern) — so importing delete.ts after update.ts silently overwrote update's captured handler in the map, and the PATCH describe block was actually invoking the DELETE handler. Fixed by keying the mock's captured-handlers map by `${method} ${path}` instead of `path` alone. This same latent bug likely exists in test/routes/ai/credentials.test.ts (PATCH/DELETE /{providerId}) — flagged as a follow-up, not fixed there since it's out of scope for HOS-64.

tasks.title: Build admin TanStack Query hooks for social credentials
tasks.description: Create `apps/admin/src/features/social-credentials/{types.ts,hooks.ts,index.ts}`, mirroring `apps/admin/src/features/ai-settings/` file-for-file: `useSocialCredentialsQuery`, `useCreateSocialCredentialMutation`, `useRotateSocialCredentialMutation`, `useUpdateSocialCredentialMutation`, `useDeleteSocialCredentialMutation`, hitting the T-026/T-027 admin routes. Type the masked credential shape (never ciphertext/iv/authTag). Component/hook tests mirroring the ai-settings hook tests if any exist, else a light render-hook test per mutation confirming cache invalidation.

subtasks.title: Define masked credential types
Implement query + 4 mutation hooks
Test cache invalidation on each mutation

qualityGate.tests: pass — 6 new tests in src/features/social-credentials/__tests__/hooks.test.ts (renderHook + QueryClientProvider wrapper, mirrors features/faqs/hooks/__tests__/useFaqs.test.ts pattern since ai-settings itself has no hook tests to mirror per the task's fallback instruction). Covers: query envelope unwrapping, no-secret-fields-on-items, and cache invalidation (queryClient.invalidateQueries spy) for all 4 mutations (create/rotate/update/delete), plus exact fetchApi path/method/body assertions per call.

tasks.decisionNote: ai-settings/ has no dedicated hook test file to mirror (grep confirmed), so followed the task's explicit fallback: a light render-hook test per mutation confirming cache invalidation, using features/faqs/hooks/__tests__/useFaqs.test.ts as the concrete pattern (mock fetchApi, QueryClientProvider wrapper, envelope-unwrap assertions) since it's the closest existing example of this exact style in the codebase. Only invalidates the single `social-credentials` query key (no settings-blob analog exists for the social vault, unlike ai-settings which also invalidates `ai-settings` on rotate/delete).
